response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional technical leadership, mentoring capability, and large-scale systems experience across 25+ years, there is a critical mismatch on the primary technical requirement. The role demands 8 years of C++ programming; this resume shows only approximately 6 months of C++ work (National Instruments, 2000-2001) from 25 years ago. Current and recent experience is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js, with no evidence of sustained C++ expertise needed to lead a cloud compute platform technically and mentor C++ engineers. The leadership strengths cannot bridge a fundamental gap in the core technical stack.
strengths: Proven technical leadership across multiple organizations, explicitly mentoring through Refactoring Dojos, Code Kata sessions, and comprehensive workshop instruction on testing, web internals, and CI/CD practices.
Extensive large-scale systems experience: handled millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby, built geo-distributed platforms, and contributed to cloud architecture using AWS CDK and Kubernetes.

Demonstrated ability to architect and own complex, ambiguous projects end-to-end: built GISCollective platform, created test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and contributed to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling.
concerns: Critical C++ experience gap: only ~6 months of C++ work documented (2000-2001), far short of the required 8 years. Career is anchored in JavaScript/TypeScript and Node.js, not systems-level languages.

No demonstrated cloud compute platform leadership: experience with general infrastructure, DevOps tooling, and cloud technologies, but no evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform specifically or mentoring cloud/DevOps teams.

Technology stack misalignment: current role emphasizes TypeScript/Node.js for product work; no recent or substantial involvement with compute technologies, distributed systems architecture, or low-level performance optimization critical to cloud platforms.
